exercises.name: Squatting Internal and External Rotation
exercises.alternateName: deep squat alternating hip open and knee drop
exercises.description:
1. When you're doing this mobility exercise, rotate the knee internally, as far as you can, towards the ground. 

2. As you rotate it externally, push out slightly with your arm to give it a little bit of a deeper stretch.

3. Repeat movement with the other knee.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Alternating Chest Press
exercises.description:
Lie on your back on a stability ball with your feet flat on the ground. 

Hold dumbbells in both hands, press one overhead while the other stays at chest level. 

Alternate pressing each dumbbell.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Alternating Superman
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down on the ball with your arms in push up position and feet touching the floor.

2.Raise one arm and the opposite leg straight up, making a straight line.

3.Lower both returning to starting position.

4.Repeat with the other arm and leg. Alternate sides with each rep.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Back Extension
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down with your chest on the ball, your hands extended forward and your legs straight out behind.

2.Raise your chest up off the ball coming to an upright position.

3.Lower down your chest returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.Do not to bounce up and down on the ball as you do each repetition.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Bent Knee Glute Bridge
exercises.alternateName: Stability Ball Proximal Hamstring Curl Hip Thruster
exercises.description:
1) Place the back of your heels on the stability ball, with arms out to the side of your body palms facedown on the floor. Keep your knee bent at a fixed joint angle, at approximately 90 degrees. 

2) With abdominals engaged, lift your hips off the ground slowly by lifting from your glutes and proximal hamstrings. Lift to the point that your body becomes a straight line from your shoulders, hips, to knees. 

3) Lower your hips to the starting position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Bird Dog
exercises.description:
Position yourself on a stability ball with your torso supported on the ball and hands or forearms on the floor for balance. 

Engage your core and extend one arm forward and the opposite leg backward simultaneously, keeping your hips and torso stable. 

Hold briefly at full extension, then return to the starting position and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Chest Fly
exercises.description:
1.Center your back on the ball, creating a bridge, holding the dumbbells up over your chest, elbows slightly bent.

2.Lower the dumbbells out and away from your body and down to shoulder level.

3.Raise the dumbbells back up over your chest, now keeping your arms straight.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Chest Press
exercises.description:
1.Center your back on the ball, creating a bridge with a dumbbell on each hand at shoulder level.

2.Press the dumbbell up until your arms are straight over your chest.

3.Lower the dumbbell back to shoulder level.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Cross Crunch
exercises.alternateName: stability ball squeeze side core crunch
exercises.description:
1)  When performing this exercise, shorten the distance between your ribs cage and your pelvis. 

2) Lie down, knees up and place the ball between your feet. 

3)  Roll up and cross your shoulder to the opposite knee.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Crunch
exercises.alternateName: Stability Ball core crunch sit up
exercises.description:
1) With your feet flat on the floor, lie on your back and center your back on the Swiss ball.

2) Crunch towards your leg, only moving your torso.

3) Return to starting position.

4) Repeat.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Dead Bug
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you'll want to keep your abdominal pulled in. 

2) If you have a small towel, place it under your lumbar spine. This will help you maintain neutral spine and give you something to push against while performing the exercise.

3) Begin lying on your back while squeezing the ball between your knees and your arms. This will be your starting position. 

4) Initiate the exercise by extending one leg, straightening the knee and hip to bring the leg towards the ground. Only go as far as you can to maintain your back against the ground. And move the opposite hand towards the ground above your head.

5) Your lumbar position must be maintained against the towel throughout the exercise.

6) Keep your abdominals drawn in and tight, and return the moving leg and arm to the starting position.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Elbow Side Plank
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to engage your glutes and your abdominals to keep a nice straight line throughout your body.

2) Try to place your elbow directly beneath your shoulder to maintain safety.

3) Prop on your side and place your elbow and forearm centered on top of the stability ball. Stack your feet on top of each other, or lay them next to each other (one in front of the other). 

4) Lift your hips, squeezing your glutes, extending your legs, and drawing abs in (engaging them). Hold this neutral spine position, actively. 

5) When you’ve completed one side, repeat it on the other.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Elevated Push Up
exercises.description:
1) Get into push up position with your hands, arms straight with one foot on the Swiss ball.

2) Lean onto the hands then place the other foot on the Swiss ball, then push up.

3) Push back returning to starting position with your arms straight.

4) Repeat.

exercises.name: Stability Ball fly to chest Press
exercises.description:
Sit on a stability ball and walk your feet forward until your upper back rests on the ball, hips lifted to form a straight line from shoulders to knees. Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended upward, palms facing each other. 

Lower the dumbbells out to your sides in a wide arc, keeping a slight bend in your elbows (chest fly). Bring the dumbbells back together to the starting position with arms fully extended, then press them straight up into a chest press.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Full Plank
exercises.alternateName: Stability Ball Staright Arm High Plank
exercises.description:
(1) Place your hands equal distance apart on the stability ball. Extend your arms until they are straight, and center the stability ball underneath you with your bodyweight equally distributed between both hands.

(2) Engage your abdominals and glutes. Slowly extend your legs out behind you one at a time. Move your body to make a straight line along your shoulders, neutral spine, hips, knees, and feet. Ensure your shoulders are directly above your hands.

(3) Hold for the prescribed duration of time, or until your form is lost (ie: not maintaining active abdominals, or not able to balance anymore).

exercises.name: Stability Ball Glute Bridge
exercises.description:
1) Place the back of heels on the stability ball, with arms out to the side of your body palms facedown on the floor. 

2) With abdominals engaged and core active, and legs straight, lift your hips off the ground slowly. Lift to the point that your body becomes a straight line with your shoulders, hips, and feet. 

3) Lower hips to starting position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Hamstring Curl with Glute Bridge
exercises.alternateName: Stability Ball Hamstring Curl and Hip Thruster
exercises.description:
1) Place the back of heels on the stability ball, with arms out to the side of your body palms facedown on the floor. 

2) With abdominals engaged and core active, and legs straight, lift your hips off the ground slowly. Lift to the point that your body becomes a straight line. 

3) While keeping your glutes and abdominals engaged, bend your knees to roll the ball towards you. 

4) Roll the ball back to the start position whilst keeping your hips up. 

5) Lower hips to starting position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Jack Knife
exercises.description:
1) Start in pushup position, but with your shins on a fitness ball. 

2) Your body should form a straight line from ankles to head. 

3) Without rounding your lower back, contract your abs and use your feet to pull the ball toward your chest by bending your knees. 

4) Pause, and return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Knee Roll Out
exercises.description:
1) Kneel on the floor with your hands on the ball.

2) Push the ball forward, rolling it along your forearms as you extend your arms, keeping your back neutral/flat.

3) Pull the ball back in to starting position.

4) Repeat.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Passovers
exercises.description:
Lie on your back with your arms extended overhead and a stability ball held between your hands. 

Lift your legs so that your knees are bent at 90 degrees or extended straight. Pass the ball from your hands to your feet, engaging your core to stabilize the movement. 

Lower your arms and legs slowly while keeping tension in your core, then pass the ball back to your hands.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Pike
exercises.description:
Start in a plank position with your feet on a stability ball. 

Engage your core to lift your hips into the air, forming a pike shape. Slowly lower back to start.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Pike to Jack Knife
exercises.description:
Start in a plank position with your shins resting on a stability ball and hands on the floor. 

Engage your core and roll the ball toward your hands by lifting your hips into a pike position. From the pike, draw your knees toward your chest in a controlled jackknife motion while keeping your upper body stable. 

Roll the ball back to the starting plank position and repeat.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Pike to Rollout
exercises.description:
Kneel with your forearms on a stability ball. 

Roll the ball forward while extending your arms and hips, keeping your core engaged. Return to start by pulling the ball back.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Plank
exercises.alternateName: Stability Ball core Plank Bosu Ball
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you want to maintain active glutes and abdominals. This will help hold neutral spine throughout.

2) When you place your hands on the ball, try to keep them in equal distance apart.

3) Raise your body up into the top of a push-up position by extending your legs back one at a time. 

4) Keep your back flat by drawing in your abdominals, and squeezing your glutes. Hold this neutral spine position actively.﻿

exercises.name: Stability Ball Push Up
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure active glutes and abdominals. This will help you keep neutral spine throughout your push ups.

2) From there, you’ll want to place your hands equal distance from the center line of the ball.

3) Slowly add weight to your hands evenly, and start engaging your upper body, abdominals, and glutes. 

4) Step back one leg at a time into a push up position. Ensure your shoulders are in line over top of your hands in this position. 

5) Lower yourself towards the ball until your torso lightly touches the ball. Do so whilst maintaining a neutral spine position (shoulder blades, abdominals, and glutes engaged).

6) Push up and keep the ball as stable as possible. 

7) Repeat the prescribed number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Roll Out
exercises.description:
1) For this exercise, keep your abdominals engaged and glutes tight. 

2) Maintain a straight line throughout your body the entire exercise. 

3) Start with your elbows underneath your shoulders. Engage your abdominals. 

4) Slowly roll the ball out and pull it back in.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Roll Up Crunch
exercises.alternateName: stability ball guided core sit up
exercises.description:
1) Lie on your back with your feet flat on the floor, knees bent, hands on the ball with your arms straight.

2) Push the ball up your leg, lifting your upper back.

3) Return to starting position.

4) Repeat.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Russian Twist
exercises.description:
1) While performing this exercise, ensure your glutes are very active. 

2) Rotate between your rib cage and your pelvis.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Single Leg Bent Knee Glute Bridge
exercises.alternateName: Stability Ball Single Leg Proximal Hamstring Curl Hip Thruster
exercises.description:
1) Place the back of your heel from one leg on the stability ball, with arms out to the side of your body palms facedown on the floor. Keep your knee bent at a fixed joint angle. 

2) With abdominals engaged and knee bent, lift your hips off the ground slowly by lifting from your glutes. Lift to the point that your body becomes a straight line from your shoulders, hips, to knee (the one on the ball). 

3) Lower your hips to the starting position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Single Leg Tuck
exercises.description:
1.Get into push up position, arms straight about shoulder width apart and one foot on the ball, while the other is on the floor, knee bent.

2.Drag the ball bringing your knee into your chest.

3.Tuck out, returning your leg to straight position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Stir The Pot
exercises.description:
Start in a plank position with your forearms resting on a stability ball and feet on the floor, forming a straight line from shoulders to heels. 

Engage your core and slowly make circular motions with your forearms on the ball, “stirring the pot.” Keep your hips and torso stable throughout the movement. 

Perform circles in one direction for the desired repetitions, then reverse the direction.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Straight Leg Dead Bug
exercises.description:
Lie on your back with a stability ball between your hands and knees. Extend one leg and the opposite arm while keeping the ball stable with the other limbs. 

Return to the start and repeat on the other side. Keep your core engaged.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Straight Leg Roll Out
exercises.description:
1.Get into plank position with your elbows placed firmly on the ball, , toes on the floor with your legs straight and feet touching the ground.

2.Push the ball forward, rolling it along your forearms as you extend your arms, keeping your back neutral/flat

3.Pull the ball back returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Torso Twist
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down on the ball with your arms in push up position and feet touching the floor.

2.Raise one arm up toward the ceiling directly in line with the hand on the floor, twisting your torso.

3.Lower back returning to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Tuck
exercises.description:
1.Get into push up position, arms straight about shoulder width apart and one foot on the ball, while the other is on the floor, knee bent.

2.Get the other leg up the ball, then drag the ball bringing your knees into your chest.

3.Tuck out, returning your legs to straight position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Walk Out
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down with the ball in your midsection and your hands and feet touching the floor.

2.Walk your hands out as you roll the ball down your body.

3.When the ball reaches your shins walk back in to the start position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Walk Out to Push Up
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down with a swiss ball in your midsection, your hands on the floor and your legs out behind.

2.Walk your hands out as you roll the ball down your body.

3.When the ball reaches your shins, perform a push up.

4.Walk back in to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: Stability Ball Weighted Crunch
exercises.description:
1.Center your back on the ball holding a dumbbell in both hands at your upper chest.

2.Lift your head and shoulders off the ball, keeping your knees bent and your feet flat.

3.Lower your head and shoulders returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.
